title: School Education: STEM Education Centres
how_it_helps: Form partnerships with EU schools/organizations under Erasmus+ to co-develop STEM Education Centres in Kenyan schools, incorporating refurbished IT equipment for digital literacy and tech education programs.

title: Tony Robbins Foundation Grant
description: The Tony Robbins Foundation Grant aims to empower individuals and organizations to improve the lives of marginalized communities, including youth, seniors, and the homeless. By providing financial support to aligned nonprofits, the foundation seeks to create impactful changes globally. Eligible organizations must be 501(c)(3) nonprofits or recognized international entities, with a mission statement that resonates with the foundation's goals. The foundation does not fund personal projects or startups.
funding_organization: Anthony Robbins Foundation (The Tony Robbins Foundation)
how_it_helps: Frame application around 'Youth Development & Leadership' by emphasizing how refurbished IT equipment builds digital skills, leadership, and opportunities for marginalized youth in African schools, aligning with empowering marginalized communities.
relevance_reasoning: Meets 4/5 minimum requirements (nonprofits, not a contest, youth focus, Africa geographic focus), but lacks direct relation to technology/digital literacy/e-waste. Youth development theme allows adaptation for tech access in schools; however, new org may face challenges as 'startups' are excluded.

title: Looking Out Foundation Grant
description: The Looking Out Foundation Grant supports social justice and humanitarian initiatives, having donated nearly $6 million to various causes globally. With an average donation of $25 per campaign, it aids organizations focused on disaster relief, racial justice, and support for marginalized communities. Eligible applicants must be tax-exempt public charities under Section 501(c)(3). The foundation prioritizes diverse funding sources and encourages organizations to adapt to changing needs, with grant allocations ranging from $5,000 to $25,000.
how_it_helps: Apply under Youth Services theme, pitching refurbishment and distribution of IT equipment to rural Kenyan schools as humanitarian aid closing digital divide for marginalized youth, requesting $10k-20k for devices and logistics.
relevance_reasoning: Meets 4/5 min requirements (nonprofits, not competition, youth focus, Africa geo). Youth Services aligns with children/youth in underserved Africa; frame digital access as support for marginalized communities. Plausible adaptation but no direct tech/education match.

title: The Hitchman Charitable Trust
description: The Hitchman Charitable Trust, established by Helen Hitchman's estate in 2014, aims to continue the philanthropic legacy of Robert and Helen Hitchman. It focuses on aiding children in need, cancer research, and supporting marginalized groups. While the Trust has a global reach, particular emphasis is placed on programs assisting children in Africa. Locally, it prioritizes grants for 501(c)(3) organizations in Washington state, especially in the greater Seattle area.
funding_organization: R And H Hitchman Charitable Trust
how_it_helps: Frame the project as a child welfare initiative by distributing refurbished IT equipment to schools in underserved Kenyan areas, directly aiding children in need through digital access and education opportunities; submit a proposal highlighting impact on African children's welfare.
relevance_reasoning: Meets 4/5 minimum requirements (nonprofits, not a contest, children focus, Africa emphasis), but lacks direct relation to technology, digital literacy, or education equipment; child welfare can be adapted to frame IT distribution as aiding children in need.

title: Rotary Club: Global Grants
description: The Rotary Club's Global Grants initiative supports significant international projects that yield sustainable results. By collaborating with local communities, Rotary clubs and districts address pressing needs across various focus areas, including health, education, and economic development. These grants, with budgets ranging from $30,000 to $400,000, enable clubs to implement impactful humanitarian projects, scholarships, and vocational training, ultimately fostering global partnerships and enhancing community welfare.
funding_organization: The Rotary Foundation Of Rotary International
how_it_helps: Partner with a local Kenyan or international Rotary club/district to co-develop and submit a Global Grant proposal for refurbishing/distributing IT equipment to establish computer labs in underserved schools, promoting digital literacy and closing digital divide under Rotary's Basic Education focus.
relevance_reasoning: Meets 4/5 min requirements (nonprofits via Rotary partnership, not competition, education focus, Africa geo, relates to education/tech via Basic Education area). Strong match for education projects in Africa; realistic path through Rotary club collaboration despite new org status.
